CreateAuditRunRequest.description:
Body for POST /audit/runs.

Empty body is valid and equivalent to a default-constructed model
(full audit, async, no LLM-cache bypass).
CreateAuditRunRequest.title: CreateAuditRunRequest

CreateBaselineRequest.description:
Body for POST /census/baselines/{name}.

`snapshot_file` is optional — when omitted the most recent snapshot
is used. The handler maps the missing-snapshot case to 422.
CreateBaselineRequest.title: CreateBaselineRequest

CreateProposalRequest.description:
Body for POST /proposals.

`write=False` is a dry-run: server builds the Proposal and computes
risk but does not persist. `write=True` persists via
ProposalService.create.
CreateProposalRequest.title: CreateProposalRequest

CreateRemediationRequest.description:
Body for POST /audit/remediations (ADR-057 D4).

`audit_run_id` is the prior `core.audit_runs` row whose findings are
to be remediated. `mode` is the aggressiveness selector — wire
vocabulary 'safe' | 'medium' | 'all' (mapped onto RemediationMode at
the facade). `write=false` is the dry-run default (ADR-014).
CreateRemediationRequest.title: CreateRemediationRequest

ProposeRequest.description:
Body for `POST /lane/{finding_id}/propose`.

The agent-authored unified diff plus the id of the `assisted.validate_diff`
run that already cleared it (an `assisted.validate_diff` action dispatched
via `POST /fix/run/{fix_id}`, tracked in `core.fix_runs`). The endpoint
re-reads that run's persisted verdict — it never trusts the caller's word
that validation passed.
ProposeRequest.title: ProposeRequest

RunFixRequest.description:
Body for POST /fix/run/{fix_id}.

Empty body is valid and equivalent to a default-constructed model
(no target file restriction, dry-run).

`params` carries action-specific kwargs (e.g. fix.docstrings's
`limit`); forwarded as **kwargs to ActionExecutor.execute.
RunFixRequest.title: RunFixRequest

RunFlowRequest.description:
Body for POST /fix/all and POST /fix/modularity.

`params` carries operation-specific kwargs (e.g. /fix/modularity's
`min_score` and `limit`); forwarded as **kwargs by route handlers
that honor them. /fix/all currently ignores params — caller can
pass them but they are unused.
RunFlowRequest.title: RunFlowRequest

info.description:
The CORE OEM API surface — the stable, versioned interface that commercial
sidecars (F-20 convergence dashboard, F-34 web dashboard, F-45 hosted
findings dashboard) and third-party OEM integrators consume to embed
CORE's constitutional governance layer.

**Stability policy:** see [ADR-087](https://github.com/DariuszNewecki/CORE/blob/main/.specs/decisions/ADR-087-oem-api-versioning-and-stability-policy.md).
**Route classification:** see [CORE-OEM-API paper](https://github.com/DariuszNewecki/CORE/blob/main/.specs/papers/CORE-OEM-API.md).

Routes marked `internal` (or absent from this spec) are CORE-operator
concerns — daemon lifecycle, autonomy-loop triggers, CI gate dispatch.
They are subject to change without notice and not part of this contract.
info.title: CORE OEM API

get.description: List active API keys for the current organisation (ORG_ADMIN+).

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Auth Api Keys Get

post.description: Generate a new API key for the current user's organisation (ORG_ADMIN+).

schema.title: Response Create Api Key Auth Api Keys Post

delete.description: Revoke an API key (ORG_ADMIN+ within their own org).

schema.title: Response Revoke Api Key Auth Api Keys  Key Id  Delete

post.description: Create an invitation link for a new user (ORG_ADMIN or PLATFORM_ADMIN).
Authenticate and set httpOnly auth cookies.
Revoke refresh token and clear auth cookies.

get.description: Return the current user's identity from their JWT (no DB hit).

post.description: Apply a password reset and revoke all existing sessions.

schema.title: Response Password Reset Confirm Auth Password Reset Confirm Post

post.description: Request a password reset token (sent via email by the caller).

schema.title: Response Password Reset Request Auth Password Reset Request Post

post.description: Exchange a valid refresh cookie for new access + refresh cookies (rotation).
Register a new user.  Returns a verification token to send via email.
Promote a user to a new role (ORG_ADMIN or PLATFORM_ADMIN only).

schema.title: Response Promote User Auth Users  User Id  Promote Post

post.description: Reactivate a suspended account (PLATFORM_ADMIN only).

schema.title: Response Reactivate User Auth Users  User Id  Reactivate Post

post.description: Suspend a user account (PLATFORM_ADMIN only).

schema.title: Response Suspend User Auth Users  User Id  Suspend Post

get.description: Verify email address via the token from the registration email.

schema.title: Response Verify Email Auth Verify Email Get

get.description: Returns 200 OK whenever the API process is responsive. Public; unauthenticated; safe to poll at high frequency.

Return metadata for every registered atomic action. Used by OEM integrators to discover what governance primitives are available for embedding. For the fix-category-only view, use `GET /v1/fix/commands`.

schema.title: Response List Actions V1 Actions Get

get.description: Return semantic capability clusters derived from the knowledge graph. `limit` caps the number of clusters returned (max 200, default 25). F-45 (hosted findings) uses cluster info for finding enrichment.

schema.title: Response Analysis Clusters V1 Analysis Clusters Get

get.description: Return the introspected CLI command tree as a structured projection — sidecar UIs render this as a discoverable command palette.

schema.title: Response Analysis Command Tree V1 Analysis Command Tree Get

get.description: Return cross-file knowledge-overlap candidates. Similar to /analysis/duplicates but at the broader knowledge level rather than symbol pair similarity. `limit` defaults to 25 (max 200).

schema.title: Response Analysis Common Knowledge V1 Analysis Common Knowledge Get

get.description: Return symbol pairs whose semantic similarity exceeds `threshold` (0.0-1.0, default 0.85). Used to surface DRY-violation candidates across the codebase.

schema.title: Response Analysis Duplicates V1 Analysis Duplicates Get

get.description: Return the test-coverage target classification (SIMPLE / COMPLEX) for in-scope source files. Used by test-generation flows to pick candidates and by F-45 dashboards to render coverage gaps.

schema.title: Response Analysis Test Targets V1 Analysis Test Targets Get

post.description: Trigger autonomous remediation of findings from a prior audit run (`audit_run_id`). `mode` selects aggressiveness — wire vocabulary is `safe` | `medium` | `all`. `write=false` is the dry-run default (ADR-014). Returns 202 + a `run_id` for the new remediation run; use `GET /v1/audit/remediations/{run_id}` to read the result. 422 if `mode` is outside the allowed vocabulary.

schema.title: Response Create Remediation Run V1 Audit Remediations Post

get.description: Read back a remediation run's persisted record by `run_id`: mode, write flag, status, timestamps, result, and error if any. Returns 404 if the run doesn't exist.

schema.title: Response Get Remediation Run V1 Audit Remediations  Run Id  Get

post.description: Start a constitutional audit run against the repo. With `wait=false` (default) returns 202 + a `run_id` to poll; the audit executes on a background task. With `wait=true` blocks and returns the full result (verdict + findings + stats) in-band (status 200). Audit duration is ~60s; clients invoking `wait=true` must set a long HTTP timeout.

schema.title: Response Create Audit Run V1 Audit Runs Post

get.description: Read back an audit run's persisted record by `run_id`: verdict + counts + timestamps + status + findings list. Returns 404 if the run doesn't exist. Findings are denormalized on `core.audit_runs.findings` per ADR-054 amendment; pre-amendment rows return an empty list.

schema.title: Response Get Audit Run V1 Audit Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Return all named baselines (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Census Baselines V1 Census Baselines Get

post.description: Promote a census snapshot to a named baseline. F-20 dashboards use baselines as reference points for the convergence trajectory. `snapshot_file` is optional; when omitted, the most recent snapshot is used. Returns 422 if no usable snapshot exists.

schema.title: Response Create Census Baseline V1 Census Baselines  Name  Post

get.description: Diff the current census state against a baseline. The optional `baseline` query param selects which named baseline to compare against; omit to use the default baseline if configured. Returns the structured delta consumed by F-20 dashboards.

schema.title: Response Census Diff V1 Census Diff Get

get.summary: Compare current state to a baseline

post.description: Trigger a CIM-0 structural census snapshot of the constitution and codebase. Returns 202 + a `run_id` to poll. F-20 (Convergence dashboard) ingests these snapshots over time to render the finding-rate vs resolution-rate trajectory.

schema.title: Response Create Census Run V1 Census Runs Post

get.description: Read back a census run's persisted record by `run_id`: snapshot, baseline_name, status, timestamps, result, error. Returns 404 if the run doesn't exist.

schema.title: Response Get Census Run V1 Census Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Return the registered V2 component inventory across the Mind/Body/Will layers. Optional `type` query filters to one component class (e.g. `analyzer`, `evaluator`, `strategist`). F-34 dashboards use this to render the component health surface.

schema.title: Response Components List V1 Components Get

get.description: Run the constitutional coverage compliance check — pass/fail verdict against the configured coverage targets. F-45 (hosted findings) renders this alongside audit findings for the same PR snapshot.

schema.title: Response Coverage Check V1 Coverage Check Get

get.description: Return modules ranked by coverage deficit below `threshold` (default 75.0, range 0-100). `limit` caps results (default 20, max 200). F-34 dashboards use this as an actionable surface for operators to triage test debt.

schema.title: Response Coverage Gaps V1 Coverage Gaps Get

get.description: Return recent coverage measurements (newest first). `limit` defaults to 30, max 500. F-20-adjacent — surfaces coverage trajectory alongside the convergence metric.

schema.title: Response Coverage History V1 Coverage History Get

get.description: Return the most recently completed pytest coverage report from `core.coverage_runs`. `format=text` (default) returns the latest text-format run; `format=html` returns the latest html-format run. 404 with a hint to POST /v1/coverage/reports if no completed report-run of the requested format exists yet. GET no longer runs pytest inline (closes #608) — POST /v1/coverage/reports kicks off a fresh run as a background task.

schema.title: Response Coverage Report V1 Coverage Report Get

get.description: Read back a coverage run's persisted record by `run_id`: status, timestamps, result payload, error. Returns 404 if no run exists with that id.

schema.title: Response Get Coverage Run V1 Coverage Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Return the constitutional coverage targets — module-level thresholds the audit gate enforces. Sidecar configuration surface.

schema.title: Response Coverage Targets V1 Coverage Targets Get

get.description: Return recent constitutional decision traces with optional filters: `session_id` scopes to one session; `agent` filters to a specific actor; `pattern` filters to a named decision pattern. `limit` defaults to 50 (max 500). Empty filters return the global newest-first stream.

schema.title: Response Decisions List V1 Decisions Get

get.description: Return aggregate pattern classification statistics across a lookback window. `days` defaults to 7 (max 365). The response shape mirrors the CLI's `inspect decisions patterns` output.

schema.title: Response Decisions Patterns V1 Decisions Patterns Get

post.description: Run the `flow.fix_code` curated sequence — a registered Flow YAML that bundles the standard fix-category atomic actions in their ADR-sanctioned execution order. Returns 202 + a `run_id`.

schema.title: Response Run Fix All V1 Fix All Post

get.description: Return metadata for atomic actions whose category is `fix`. Use `GET /v1/actions` for the unfiltered view across every category.

schema.title: Response List Fix Commands V1 Fix Commands Get

post.description: Bootstrap an Intent Representation (IR) scaffold file of the requested `kind` (`triage` or `log`). Synchronous — returns the relative path that was written. No background task; no core.fix_runs row. Returns 422 if `kind` is outside the closed vocabulary.

schema.title: Response Run Fix Ir V1 Fix Ir Post

post.description: Run the modularity remediation cycle — a Python-level workflow (ModularityRemediationService), not a Flow YAML — so there's no flow_id to validate. `params` supports `min_score` + `limit` for candidate selection. Returns 202 + a `run_id`.

schema.title: Response Run Fix Modularity V1 Fix Modularity Post

post.description: Run an atomic action from the registry by `fix_id` (e.g. `fix.format`, `fix.imports`, `fix.docstrings`). Returns 422 if `fix_id` is unknown — the response body includes a registered ids count. Valid ids return 202 + a `run_id` to poll. `write=false` is the dry-run default (ADR-014). `params` carries action-specific kwargs (e.g. `fix.docstrings`'s `limit`) and is forwarded as `**kwargs` to ActionExecutor.execute.

schema.title: Response Run Fix V1 Fix Run  Fix Id  Post

get.description: Read back a fix run's persisted record by `run_id`. The response shape matches ADR-055 D2: run_id, status, fix_id, kind, write, result, error. `result` is the JSONB ActionResult payload written by the background task; null while the run is still pending or executing. Returns 404 if no run exists with that id.

schema.title: Response Get Fix Run V1 Fix Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Return the capability registry from the knowledge graph. Sidecar consumers (F-34 web dashboard) use this to render the capability tree and to anchor proposal/action views against the constitutional capability taxonomy.

schema.title: Response List Capabilities V1 Knowledge Capabilities Get

get.description: Return findings delegated for human-gated remediation — the Assisted Remediation Lane work queue. A delegated finding is one at `status=indeterminate` with `resolution_mechanism=human` (ADR-109). Ordered oldest-first; the `limit` query param caps the response size. The context-bundle exporter (#653) will enrich each entry later; for now the raw finding payload is returned.

schema.title: Response List Delegated Findings V1 Lane Get

get.summary: List delegated findings (the assisted-lane work queue)

get.description: Return the oldest delegated finding — the lane's 'pull next work' surface — or 404 if the lane is empty. The rich context bundle (#653) will enrich this later; for now the raw finding is returned. Declared before /{finding_id} so 'next' is not captured as an id.

schema.title: Response Next Delegated Finding V1 Lane Next Get

get.summary: Pull the next delegated finding (FIFO head)

get.description: Return one delegated finding by id (same governor-inbox predicate as the list) enriched with the #653 context bundle — rule rationale, whether the rule is still in the active registry, and the remediation-map guidance. 404 if it is not a live lane item — already worked, resolved, or never delegated.

schema.title: Response Get Delegated Finding V1 Lane  Finding Id  Get

get.summary: Get a single delegated finding with its context bundle

post.description: Stamp a delegated finding as in-progress by an external agent (ADR-109 §2). Claiming does not change the finding's status — it stays a live lane item — it only records who is working it so it is visibly tracked, not parked. 404 if it is not a live lane item.

schema.title: Response Claim Delegated Finding V1 Lane  Finding Id  Claim Post

post.summary: Mark a delegated finding as being worked

post.description: Create a human-gated multi-file proposal from an agent-authored diff that has already cleared `assisted.validate_diff` (ADR-109 D3/D4). This endpoint is DB-only: it re-reads the persisted verdict of the named validation run from `core.fix_runs` (it never trusts the caller's claim that validation passed), confirms the submitted patch matches the bytes that were validated, then routes proposal creation and finding deferral through the Will-layer LaneService. The existing `proposals approve`/`execute` path drives the rest.

schema.title: Response Propose Diff V1 Lane  Finding Id  Propose Post

post.summary: Ingest a validated agent diff as a human-gated proposal

get.description: List proposals, optionally filtered by `status`. When `status` is omitted, returns proposals awaiting approval. The `limit` query param caps the response size. F-34 (web dashboard) consumes this endpoint to render the proposal queue.

schema.title: Response List Proposals V1 Proposals Get

post.description: Build a proposal from a goal + action sequence, compute its risk, and (when `write=true`) persist it via ProposalService. With `write=false` (default) the proposal is constructed and risk-scored in-memory but NOT written to the database — useful for dry-run validation. Returns the proposal's `to_dict()` shape either way.

schema.title: Response Create Proposal V1 Proposals Post

get.description: Return a proposal by `proposal_id`. Returns 404 if no proposal exists with that id.

schema.title: Response Get Proposal V1 Proposals  Proposal Id  Get

post.description: Approve a proposal awaiting governance review. `approval_authority` is non-omittable per URS NFR.5 and validated against the `proposal_approval_authority` closed set inside ProposalStateManager. Returns 404 if the proposal doesn't exist; 400 if the authority is outside the closed vocabulary or the proposal is in a non-approvable state.

schema.title: Response Approve Proposal V1 Proposals  Proposal Id  Approve Post

post.description: Execute an approved proposal as a governor-direct override. Defaults to dry-run; pass `{"write": true}` to apply changes. The proposal must be in approved status; ProposalExecutor manages its own session lifecycle.

schema.title: Response Execute Proposal V1 Proposals  Proposal Id  Execute Post

post.description: Reject a proposal with a reason. Per ADR-010 §7a, rejection is symmetric with `mark_failed`: any findings parked at `deferred_to_proposal` are revived (flipped to `awaiting_reaudit` per ADR-045) so the audit sensor re-adjudicates them. Response includes `revived_count` for the operator. Returns 404 if the proposal doesn't exist.

schema.title: Response Reject Proposal V1 Proposals  Proposal Id  Reject Post

get.description: Return files exceeding the modularity threshold, highest score first. `min_score` filters to files at or above the given score (0-200); `limit` caps results (default 50, max 500). F-34 dashboards use this as an actionable refactor backlog view.

schema.title: Response Refactor Candidates V1 Refactor Candidates Get

get.description: Read back a refactor run's persisted record by `run_id`: status, timestamps, captured proposal_ids in `result`, error. Returns 404 if no run exists with that id.

schema.title: Response Get Refactor Run V1 Refactor Runs  Run Id  Get

get.description: Return the per-file modularity score for `file` (relative path). Returns 404 if the file is unknown / missing; analyzable files return the full details payload from the modularity engine.

schema.title: Response Refactor Score V1 Refactor Score Get

get.description: Return the aggregate modularity-score distribution across the codebase — histogram + percentile summary used for trend analysis and dashboard rendering.

schema.title: Response Refactor Stats V1 Refactor Stats Get

get.description: Return the constitutional modularity score threshold — files scoring above this are refactor candidates. Sidecar configuration surface.

schema.title: Response Refactor Threshold V1 Refactor Threshold Get

get.description: Return recent constitutional refusal records (RefusalResult outcomes from components that declined to proceed). Optional filters: `type` narrows to one RefusalResult category (extraction_failed / low_confidence / boundary_violation / quality_threshold); `session` scopes to one session. F-34 renders these in the governance-trail view.

schema.title: Response Refusals List V1 Refusals Get

get.description: Return refusal statistics grouped by RefusalResult type across a lookback window. `days` defaults to 7 (max 365).

schema.title: Response Refusals Stats V1 Refusals Stats Get

get.description: Semantic search across the capability registry via the Will cognitive service. `q` is the query (min length 1, required). `limit` caps results (default 10, max 200). Returns capabilities ranked by semantic similarity to the query.

schema.title: Response Search Capabilities V1 Search Capabilities Get

get.description: Fuzzy substring search over the CLI command registry (ADR-057 D5). `q` is the search string (min length 1, required). `limit` caps results (default 25, max 200).

schema.title: Response Search Commands V1 Search Commands Get

get.description: Read-only DB-state snapshot: connection liveness + schema introspection summary. F-34 (web dashboard) uses this for the worker-health surface.

schema.title: Response Status Db V1 Status Db Get

get.description: Return a consolidated drift snapshot for symbols and/or vectors. `scope=symbols` and `scope=vectors` filter to one axis; `scope=all` (default) returns both. F-20 (convergence dashboard) consumes this to render real-time drift state alongside the convergence trajectory.

schema.title: Response Status Drift V1 Status Drift Get
